title: Make a Simple Savings Plan
summary: Set a savings target and decide how much to set aside regularly.
learnerPromise: You will be able to set a realistic savings goal and break it into small, regular steps.
whyItMatters: Saving money becomes easier when you have a clear target and a plan, even small amounts add up over time and help you handle bigger expenses without stress.
realLifeUses: Saving $200 for a new phone over four months by setting aside $50 each month
Building a small emergency fund for unexpected car repairs or medical costs
Setting aside money for a concert ticket before it goes on sale
Saving for holiday gifts so you are not short on cash in December
result: You have a written savings plan with a target amount, timeline, and regular contribution.
youWillLearn: Pick a specific, realistic savings target
Calculate how much you need to save per week or month
Choose a place to keep savings separate from spending money
Adjust the plan if your income or expenses change
miniChallenge: Pick something you want to buy within the next six months and create a weekly savings plan to afford it.
steps: Write down one specific thing you want to save for and its estimated cost.
Decide when you want to reach your goal and count how many weeks or months that is.
Divide the total amount by the number of weeks or months to find your regular savings amount.
Look at your usual income and spending to check if the amount feels realistic.
Choose where to put the savings, such as a separate savings account or an envelope, and schedule when you will transfer the money.
completionCriteria: You wrote down a specific savings goal with a dollar amount and a deadline
You calculated a regular savings amount and confirmed it fits your budget
commonProblems: Setting a goal that is too aggressive and feeling discouraged
Unexpected expenses that interrupt the plan
Keeping savings in the same account as spending money, making it too easy to use
tips: Start smaller than you think; consistency matters more than the amount
Name your savings account after your goal to make it feel more real
If you miss one week, do not give up; just continue the next week
